renderReport.fallbackPolicy: helm template and kustomize build are mandatory when their tools are available; static-template-render is allowed only when the corresponding tool is absent and the report records an explicit fallbackReasons entry
renderReport.schemaValidationPolicy: kubeconform and kubeval are optional CI validators; when available they must run against rendered Helm and Kustomize output, and when absent the report must mark schemaValidationStatus as tool-unavailable with a fallback reason

renderModes.fallbackReason: helm not installed in the local or CI environment

adopterRolloutProof.policy: Adopters may treat cloud-native assets as production rollout evidence only when Helm or static fallback rendering, Kustomize overlays, policy resources, schema-tool status, and rollout gates are all explicit.

rolloutProfiles.renderMode: helm-template when helm is available, static-template-render only when helm is unavailable
rolloutProfiles.adopterAction: Render the default Helm chart locally before using generated Kubernetes assets as smoke evidence.
rolloutProfiles.rollbackAction: Keep direct Kubernetes manifests or the previous chart version active when Helm output omits required resources.

rolloutProfiles.renderMode: helm-template when helm is available, static-template-render only when helm is unavailable
rolloutProfiles.adopterAction: Render production values and verify ServiceMonitor, HPA, PDB, and NetworkPolicy before release promotion.
rolloutProfiles.rollbackAction: Pin the previous chart or disable production-only policy resources when rendered output or schema validation fails.

rolloutProfiles.renderMode: kustomize when available, checked rendered golden when unavailable
rolloutProfiles.adopterAction: Use the production overlay and rendered golden as fallback evidence only when tool availability is recorded.
rolloutProfiles.rollbackAction: Keep the previous overlay applied until Kustomize output and the rendered golden converge.

policyResourceRequirements.adopterAction: Verify metrics port and scrape interval before enabling production monitoring.
policyResourceRequirements.rollbackAction: Disable ServiceMonitor publication when metrics endpoint or labels drift from the rendered output.

policyResourceRequirements.adopterAction: Verify min replicas, max replicas, and CPU utilization target before enabling autoscaling.
policyResourceRequirements.rollbackAction: Pin replica counts or disable HPA when scaling policy is incomplete.

policyResourceRequirements.adopterAction: Verify minAvailable before allowing voluntary disruptions in production namespaces.
policyResourceRequirements.rollbackAction: Remove or relax the PDB when rollout safety conflicts with cluster capacity.

policyResourceRequirements.adopterAction: Verify ingress, egress, and DNS allowance before applying namespace isolation.
policyResourceRequirements.rollbackAction: Apply the previous NetworkPolicy or disable isolation when required traffic is blocked.

adopterRolloutProof.toolFallbackPolicy: Helm and Kustomize are required when available; kubeconform and kubeval are optional CI validators whose unavailable status must be written to the render report.

renderedGoldens.name: static-production
renderedGoldens.producedBy: cat k8s/deployment.yaml k8s/servicemonitor.yaml k8s/hpa.yaml k8s/pdb.yaml k8s/networkpolicy.yaml

p10CloudNativeAdoptionProof.policy: P10 treats cloud-native adoption proof as a joined contract across rendered Kubernetes assets, production-orders topology, runtime SLO evidence, and rollback notes. Rendered YAML alone is not enough for production adoption.

proofChains.surface: Helm, Kustomize, ServiceMonitor, HPA, PDB, and NetworkPolicy render evidence
proofChains.adopterAction: Render production assets, inspect explicit fallback reasons, and verify policy resources before applying Helm or Kustomize output.
proofChains.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the previous chart release or manifest active when render output, schema validation, or required policy resources are missing.

proofChains.surface: production-orders memory and Docker-backed reference topology
proofChains.adopterAction: Run memory smoke locally and Docker-backed topology rehearsal when dependencies are available before changing traffic routing.
proofChains.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep memory mode or the previous deployment active when dependency topology evidence is skipped or cannot explain fallback.

proofChains.surface: golden signals, ServiceMonitor scrape evidence, runtime SLOs, and trace correlation
proofChains.adopterAction: Review latency, errors, traffic, saturation, cache, governance decisions, and trace correlation before promotion.
proofChains.rollbackOrEscalation: Hold rollout or route back to the previous deployment when SLO evidence cannot explain runtime regressions.

proofChains.surface: adopter rollback decision across deployment, topology, and incident evidence
proofChains.adopterAction: Use adoption risk, runbook drills, topology output, and release evidence to decide whether to promote, pin, or route back.
proofChains.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin the previous release, keep old routing active, or disable new policy resources when adoption proof is incomplete.

p10CloudNativeAdoptionProof.runtimeEvidencePolicy: Runtime reports under .tmp-test or .aiflow are local evidence only; durable adoption proof must stay in docs/reference manifests and committed production assets.
p10CloudNativeAdoptionProof.promotionPolicy: Promote cloud-native adoption claims only when render proof, reference topology proof, runtime SLO proof, rollback proof, and P1 growth gates all pass.
